blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: I don't think I'm better than you. I just don't have a problem with you.
He didn't expect that. The two guys behind him shifted. Daniel looked like he was trying to find the next thing to say and couldn't.
I turned around. Walked back through the side door. Let the door close behind me.
He said something behind me but I didn't catch the words. Maybe that was the point.
I pulled out my phone and sent two words. He was inside at practice. Side exit. I watched Daniel clock it and something shifted in his face — like he'd just done math he didn't like.
I said nothing. Held the eye contact. Didn't look away, didn't move. The two guys behind him exchanged a look. Daniel didn't know what to do with silence.
And then a car pulled up to the curb.
Not fast. Not dramatic. Just — there. A sedan, idling. The window already down.
That was all he said. Just my name. And something about the way he said it — or the way he was looking at Daniel when he said it — made everything go still.
